heading: Adjustment of House of Representatives allowances by Committee on House Oversight
label: 4313 Adjustment of House of Representatives allowances by Committee on House Oversight
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.section 57 of this titleSection is based on House Resolution No. 457, Ninety-second Congress, , which was enacted into permanent law by .July 21, 1971Pub. L. 92–184

Amendments1999—Subsec. (a)(1), (2). substituted “all aspects of official mail” for “all aspects of the Official Mail Allowance”.Pub. L. 106–571996— amended section generally. Prior to amendment, section consisted of subsecs. (a) and (b) authorizing Committee on House Administration to adjust certain allowances for Members, committees, and officers of House of Representatives.Pub. L. 104–186

Change of NameCommittee on House Oversight of House of Representatives changed to Committee on House Administration of House of Representatives by House Resolution No. 5, One Hundred Sixth Congress, .Jan. 6, 1999

children.text: For Members of the House of Representatives, the Members’ Representational Allowance, including all aspects of official mail within the jurisdiction of the Committee under .section 503 of this title

For committees, the Speaker, the Majority and Minority Leaders, the Clerk, the Sergeant at Arms, and the Chief Administrative Officer, allowances for official mail (including all aspects of official mail within the jurisdiction of the Committee under ), stationery, and telephone and telegraph and other communications.section 503 of this title

children.heading: Provision specified
children.label: b Provision specified
children.text: The provision of law referred to in subsection (a) is .section 4314 of this title

children.heading: “Member of the House of Representatives” defined
children.label: c “Member of the House of Representatives” defined
children.text: As used in this section, the term “Member of the House of Representatives” means a Representative in, or a Delegate or Resident Commissioner to, the Congress.
